evals.prompt: Design pavement rehabilitation for a 10-year-old trunk road in Uganda. Condition data: visual survey shows cracking on 15% of the surface area, maximum rut depth 12mm, IRI = 13. Deflection survey (Benkelman beam, temperature-corrected to 35C): mean deflection 0.8mm, standard deviation 0.2mm. The original pavement SN was 3.5. Future design traffic is 5 million ESAs over the next 10 years. Design CBR is 8%. Determine whether overlay or reconstruction is needed, and if overlay, calculate the required thickness.
evals.expected_output: Condition: IRI 13 = Fair, rehabilitation appropriate. Reconstruction triggers check: cracking 15% < 40%, rutting 12mm < 25mm, deflection 0.8mm < 2.0mm -- NO reconstruction triggers. Overlay is appropriate. RRD = 0.8 + 2*0.2 = 1.2mm. SN_new approximately 3.6 (from AASHTO equation). SN_eff approximately 2.5-3.0 (from deflection). Overlay = (SN_new - SN_eff)/0.35 * 25.4, approximately 50-80mm AC overlay.

assertions.text: Uses deflection procedure (Benkelman beam data available)
Checks reconstruction triggers before proceeding to overlay design
Calculates overlay thickness using (SN_new - SN_eff)/a1 formula with a1 = 0.35
Overlay thickness is in practical range 40-100mm for AC

evals.prompt: Assess rehabilitation needs for a road in the UK with similar condition data: cracking 15%, rutting 12mm, IRI 13. What assessment framework should be used?
evals.expected_output: Notes that UK uses DMRB HD 30/HD 32 for maintenance/rehabilitation assessment, not MoWT Part IV. UK uses SCANNER surveys and SCRIM data. Assessment framework is fundamentally different. Flags that no local HD 30/HD 32 documents are available. Describes UK approach conceptually.

assertions.text: References HD 30/HD 32 as UK rehabilitation assessment standards (not MoWT Part IV)
Notes that local HD 30/HD 32 documents are not available

evals.prompt: A trunk road in Uganda has severe structural failure: mean deflection 2.5mm (Benkelman beam), cracking on 45% of the surface, rutting 30mm, and DCP shows base CBR has dropped to 25%. The original design was a D1 structure with natural gravel base. Determine the appropriate rehabilitation intervention.
evals.expected_output: Multiple reconstruction triggers are met: deflection 2.5mm > 2.0mm threshold, cracking 45% > 40% threshold, rutting 30mm > 25mm threshold, base CBR 25% < 30% (base layer has failed). Overlay is NOT appropriate -- reconstruction is needed. Escalation: full reconstruction with subgrade investigation before redesign. Consider recycling existing base material if properties allow.

assertions.text: Identifies ALL reconstruction triggers: deflection >2.0mm, cracking >40%, rutting >25mm, base failure
Recommends reconstruction (NOT overlay) due to severe structural failure
Raises escalation for subgrade investigation before reconstruction design
Does NOT attempt to calculate overlay thickness (reconstruction is the correct intervention)

evals.prompt: A 30 km section of Kenya Class B road shows average IRI of 6.5 m/km, rutting of 15mm, and 30% moderate cracking. Design a rehabilitation strategy.
evals.expected_output: References RDM 5.2 as the Kenya rehabilitation standard. Uses Kenya condition thresholds: IRI 6.5 = Poor (rehabilitation required), rutting 15mm = moderate, cracking 30% moderate = borderline. Checks reconstruction triggers -- none met. Recommends overlay with crack mitigation measures. Calculates overlay thickness. Specifies pre-overlay repairs and drainage assessment.

assertions.text: References Kenya RDM 5.2 as the applicable rehabilitation standard
Uses Kenya RDM 5.2 condition thresholds (not Uganda MoWT Part IV thresholds)
Recommends overlay or reconstruction with technical justification based on condition data
Specifies pre-overlay repairs and/or drainage improvements as part of rehabilitation strategy
